methodsAttempted.query: site:wvu.edu West Virginia University generative AI policy ChatGPT academic integrity teaching guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official IT guidance, Academic Integrity PDF FAQ, Faculty Senate syllabus statements, and a WVU E-News announcement.

methodsAttempted.query: site:wvu.edu WVU AI approved tools privacy data security generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search confirmed the IT guidance as the primary data/security source and returned an official E-News duplicate summary.

methodsAttempted.query: site:library.wvu.edu OR site:libguides.wvu.edu WVU artificial intelligence citation ChatGPT library guide
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official WVU Libraries ChatGPT guide pages, including the Citation Styles and Policy page.
Verified official WVU domains/subdomains used for claims: it.wvu.edu, academicintegrity.wvu.edu, facultysenate.wvu.edu, and libguides.wvu.edu.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found current WVU AI guidance for administrative operations, Academic Integrity student FAQ guidance, Faculty Senate AI syllabus guidance, and WVU Libraries citation resources. Claims are scoped to the source context and do not treat the news announcement or optional sample syllabus language as broader binding policy.

artifacts.sourceTitle: WVU adopts formal guidance around responsible use of AI in administrative operations
artifacts.queryUsed: site:wvu.edu WVU AI approved tools privacy data security generative AI
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official WVU E-News announcement summarized and linked to the formal IT guidance; rejected for direct claim extraction because the formal guidance page is the stronger source.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The University has adopted formal guidelines for faculty, staff and students to help ensure that generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools are responsibly integrated into administrative operations.
artifacts.notes: Official WVU news source was useful for discovery, but direct claims use the linked formal guidance page instead.

targets.sourceTitle: Use of Generative AI for Administrative Purposes at WVU

stopConditions: Stop after official WVU sources provide current AI-specific administrative, academic integrity, teaching, and citation guidance.
Do not use unofficial, repository, vendor, media, or search-snippet-only sources as evidence.

artifacts.title: West Virginia University AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Staged review found official WVU sources covering administrative generative AI guidance, sensitive-data restrictions and IT purchase review, student academic-integrity expectations for AI use, Faculty Senate AI syllabus language, and WVU Libraries citation-style resources. All claims remain agent-reviewed candidates and are not canonical public facts.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Faculty Senate sample AI statements are optional syllabus language and are not treated as a university-wide default rule beyond their stated context.
The WVU E-News announcement was rejected for claim extraction because it is an announcement pointing to the formal guidance page.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
